title: Rhea Seehorn's Golden Globe Win Breaks Pattern of Emmy Repeats
text: Rhea Seehorn's victory in the TV Drama Actress category at the 2026 Golden Globes stands out as the sole fresh winner among the evening's awards. The "Pluribus" star claimed the only Golden Globe that didn't simply repeat a previous Emmy winner. Of 11 Golden Globe TV awards across drama, comedy, and limited series categories, 10 went to prior Emmy victors or nominees. As the article notes, "The only fresh winner at the 2026 Golden Globes to prove we weren't watching a rerun of the Emmys was Rhea Seehorn." Seehorn competed against previous Emmy winner Britt Lower ("Severance") and four other nominees, triumphing in a category dominated by returning contenders. With back-to-back Golden Globe and Critics Choice Awards victories, Seehorn has solidified her position as the lead contender heading into September's Emmy Awards. Notably, the acclaimed performer has yet to win an Emmy despite two prior supporting nominations for "Better Call Saul" and one for the short-form series "Cooper's Bar." Of the major Golden Globe winners, only three will compete at the 2026 Emmys: "The Pitt," "Hacks," and "Pluribus," as limited series like "Adolescence" and "Dying for Sex" won't return to Emmy eligibility.
